questions.scenario: You want to obtain the Getsu Sen.
questions.question: Which combo route grants Getsu Sen?
questions.explanation: Hakaze → Jinpu → Gekko is the Jinpu path, which grants Getsu Sen from Gekko. The Shifu path grants Ka Sen.

questions.scenario: Your Fugetsu buff is about to expire. Fuka has 20 seconds remaining.
questions.question: Which buff should you prioritize refreshing?
questions.explanation: Fugetsu (+13% damage) should be prioritized over Fuka (+13% haste). Damage affects all your abilities.

questions.scenario: You have Getsu and Ka Sen. You need Setsu Sen.
questions.question: Which ability grants Setsu Sen?
questions.explanation: Yukikaze is the only finisher that grants Setsu Sen. It's a 2-hit combo (Hakaze → Yukikaze) without a positional.

questions.scenario: You're starting a fight. What should you establish first?
questions.question: What's the optimal buff priority in the opener?
questions.explanation: In the opener, you want both buffs up quickly. The standard opener establishes both Fugetsu and Fuka before your first Midare.

questions.scenario: At level 92+, your combo starter changes.
questions.question: What replaces Hakaze at level 92?
questions.explanation: Gyofu replaces Hakaze at level 92, dealing higher potency. It's still used the same way as your combo starter.

questions.scenario: Your Kenki gauge is at 85. You're about to use a combo that generates 10 Kenki.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: At 85 Kenki with 10 more coming, you'll overcap (95 after, missing 5). Use Shinten (25 cost) to make room.

questions.scenario: Single target fight. You have 50 Kenki.
questions.question: Which Kenki spender should you use?
questions.explanation: Shinten is the single-target Kenki spender. Kyuten is for AoE (3+ targets). Senei costs 25 Kenki and has a cooldown.

questions.scenario: You have 3 Meditation stacks. When should you use Shoha?
questions.question: What's the optimal Shoha timing?
questions.explanation: Use Shoha during burst windows when possible. At 3 stacks you can't build more, so spend it to avoid waste.

questions.scenario: You're about to use Ikishoten. Your Kenki is at 60.
questions.question: What's wrong with this situation?
questions.explanation: Ikishoten grants 50 Kenki. At 60 Kenki, using it would put you at 110, wasting 10 gauge. Spend to 50 or below first.

questions.scenario: You gain Meditation stacks from using Iaijutsu.
questions.question: How many Meditation stacks does one Iaijutsu grant?
questions.explanation: Each Iaijutsu grants 1 Meditation stack. You need 3 Iaijutsu casts to get Shoha ready.

questions.scenario: You're in a single-target fight. Higanbana is not on the boss. You have 1 Sen.
questions.question: Should you use Higanbana now?
options: No - build to 3 Sen for Midare
questions.explanation: Higanbana is worth applying only if the boss will live long enough for the DoT to tick (60s duration). In short fights, Midare is better.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Sen (Getsu and Ka). The boss is at 5% HP.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Even at low boss HP, building to 3 Sen for Midare Setsugekka is worth it. Tenka Goken is only for multi-target.

questions.scenario: 3 enemies are grouped together. You have 2 Sen.
questions.question: Which Iaijutsu should you use?
questions.explanation: Tenka Goken hits all nearby enemies. At 3 targets, using 2 Sen for AoE damage is more efficient than building for single-target Midare.

questions.scenario: Higanbana has 5 seconds remaining on the boss.
questions.question: Should you refresh it now?
questions.explanation: Refresh DoTs at 3 seconds or less to avoid clipping (losing ticks). Refreshing at 5s wastes potential damage.

questions.scenario: You have 1 Sen in single-target. Higanbana is already on the boss with 40s remaining.
questions.question: What should you do with this Sen?
questions.explanation: Don't overwrite Higanbana early. Build to 3 Sen for Midare Setsugekka instead - it's your primary damage tool.

questions.scenario: You just used Midare Setsugekka. Tsubame-gaeshi is available.
questions.question: What should you do next?
questions.explanation: Tsubame-gaeshi should be used immediately after Iaijutsu. Kaeshi: Setsugekka is massive damage - never skip it.

questions.scenario: Meikyo Shisui is ready. You have 0 Sen and need to burst soon.
questions.question: What's the optimal Meikyo usage?
questions.explanation: Use each finisher once to get all 3 Sen types (Getsu, Ka, Setsu) for immediate Midare Setsugekka.

questions.scenario: During Meikyo, you already have Getsu and Ka Sen. You have 1 Meikyo stack left.
questions.question: Which finisher should you use?
questions.explanation: You're missing Setsu Sen. Use Yukikaze to complete your Sen collection for Midare Setsugekka.

questions.scenario: You used Tenka Goken in AoE. What Kaeshi ability appears?
questions.question: What follows Tenka Goken?
questions.explanation: Tsubame-gaeshi creates Kaeshi versions matching your Iaijutsu. Tenka Goken → Kaeshi: Goken for AoE.

questions.scenario: Meikyo Shisui has a 55 second cooldown.
questions.question: How does this affect your rotation?
questions.explanation: At 55s cooldown, Meikyo roughly aligns with 60s burst windows. Plan usage around party buffs.

questions.scenario: Ikishoten is ready. Your Kenki is at 30.
questions.question: Is it safe to use Ikishoten?
questions.explanation: At 30 Kenki, Ikishoten (+50) puts you at 80. That's safe. Never use Ikishoten above 50 Kenki.

questions.scenario: You used Ikishoten. What GCD should come next?
questions.question: What's the correct sequence?
questions.explanation: Ikishoten grants Ogi Namikiri Ready. Use Ogi Namikiri immediately for your highest potency GCD.

questions.scenario: You just used Kaeshi: Namikiri. Zanshin is now available.
questions.question: What is Zanshin?
questions.explanation: Zanshin is an enhanced oGCD Kenki spender available after the Ogi Namikiri sequence. Big damage!

questions.scenario: Senei (high-potency oGCD) is ready. You're in burst window.
questions.question: When should you use Senei?
questions.explanation: Senei deals high damage. Use it during burst windows when party buffs are active for maximum effect.

questions.scenario: Ikishoten has a 120 second cooldown.
questions.question: How does this align with party bursts?
questions.explanation: 120s = 2 minutes. Ikishoten aligns perfectly with standard 2-minute raid buff windows.

questions.scenario: You need to use Gekko for Getsu Sen.
questions.question: Where should you stand?
questions.explanation: Gekko has a rear positional. Stand behind the boss for bonus potency.

questions.scenario: You need to use Kasha for Ka Sen.
questions.question: Where should you stand?
questions.explanation: Kasha has a flank positional. Stand at the boss's side for bonus potency.

questions.scenario: Boss is against the wall. You can't reach rear for Gekko.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: True North removes positional requirements for 10 seconds. Use it when you physically can't reach the correct position.

questions.scenario: You missed Gekko's positional. The attack still went off.
questions.question: What happened?
questions.explanation: Missing a positional means the attack still works but deals reduced potency. You lose the bonus damage.

questions.scenario: True North has 2 charges with 45s recharge each.
questions.question: How should you manage True North usage?
options: Use one, keep one in reserve
questions.explanation: Keep at least one charge available for unexpected situations. Don't cap at 2 charges, but don't waste them either.

questions.scenario: Party raid buffs are coming in 5 seconds. Ikishoten is ready.
questions.question: When should you use Ikishoten?
questions.explanation: Align Ikishoten with party raid buffs for maximum damage during Ogi Namikiri and the entire burst sequence.

questions.scenario: Boss is about to become untargetable for 20 seconds. You have 3 Sen.
questions.question: What should you do with your Sen?
questions.explanation: Use Midare before downtime if possible. Only use Hagakure if you can't get Midare off in time - Midare damage > Kenki value.

questions.scenario: 4 enemies are grouped. You're doing the AoE rotation.
questions.question: What Sen types can you build with AoE combos?
questions.explanation: Fuko → Mangetsu grants Getsu. Fuko → Oka grants Ka. You cannot build Setsu Sen in AoE - it requires Yukikaze.

questions.scenario: Fugetsu has 3 seconds left. Meikyo Shisui is available.
questions.question: Should you use Meikyo to refresh Fugetsu?
options: Only if Fuka is also low
questions.explanation: Meikyo can quickly refresh buffs in emergencies. Gekko refreshes Fugetsu without needing the full combo.

questions.scenario: You have 3 Sen. Phase transition is in 2 GCDs.
questions.question: You can't use Midare in time. What's your best option?
questions.explanation: Hagakure converts Sen to Kenki (10 per Sen). 30 Kenki is better than losing Sen entirely to phase transition.
